descriptions: to aim at someone or something with a gun or camera
to start to give all your attention to a particular person or thing
examples: The newspapers have zeroed in on his private life.
They can help us zero in on problems that otherwise might not be found.
highlighted_examples: The newspapers have [zeroed] [in] [on] his private life.
They can help us [zero] [in] [on] problems that otherwise might not be found.

descriptions: to deal with or complete something very quickly
examples: I zipped through the first three questions in ten minutes.
highlighted_examples: I [zipped] [through] the first three questions in ten minutes.

descriptions: to be closed by means of a zip
to close a zip on a piece of clothing for someone
to close something that has a zip
examples: Do you want me to zip you up?
He's zipped up in a body bag, taken down to the mortuary and that's that, leaving his distraught colleagues determined to avenge his death.
It zips up at the back.
It's why their hoods are zipped up.
She tries to zip up his suit to hide the offending hair but can only manage a couple of centimetres.
highlighted_examples: Do you want me to [zip] you [up]?
He's [zipped] [up] in a body bag, taken down to the mortuary and that's that, leaving his distraught colleagues determined to avenge his death.
She tries to [zip] [up] his suit to hide the offending hair but can only manage a couple of centimetres.

descriptions: to stop paying attention and become unaware of what is happening around you
examples: I tend to zone out during long meetings.
He zoned out and missed what the teacher said.
highlighted_examples: I tend to [zone] [out] during long meetings.
He [zoned] [out] and missed what the teacher said.

descriptions: to make something appear larger or closer, especially on a screen
to focus closely on a detail
examples: You can zoom in to see the details more clearly.
The camera zoomed in on her face.
highlighted_examples: You can [zoom] [in] to see the details more clearly.

descriptions: to make something appear smaller or further away on a screen
to consider a situation more broadly
examples: Zoom out to see the entire map.
Sometimes you need to zoom out and look at the bigger picture.
highlighted_examples: Zoom [out] to see the entire map.
Sometimes you need to [zoom] [out] and look at the bigger picture.
